target.title: fix(update): make completion-cache spawn timeout configurable
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS` to override the 30-second `spawnSync` timeout used by `tryWriteCompletionCache` (post-update auto-regen) and `generateCompletionCache` (manual `openclaw doctor completion`). Default behavior unchanged.
- Extracts the env-var read into `resolveCompletionCacheWriteTimeoutMs()` in `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts` so both spawn sites share the same lookup. Removes two duplicated `30_000` constants.
- Adds eight unit tests covering unset / empty / whitespace / non-numeric / zero / negative / positive / whitespace-trimmed env-var values.
- CHANGELOG entry under `2026.5.2 > Fixes`.

## Motivation

Defensive engineering around the post-update completion-cache auto-regen path. 30 seconds is a comfortable budget on a development laptop, but on Pi-class hardware, throttled containers, and low-end VPSes it can be tight: any slowdown in the spawned `openclaw completion --write-state` surfaces as `Completion cache update failed: timed out after 30s. Shell tab-completion may be stale; refresh manually with: openclaw completion --write-state`.

The acute version of this problem — three full plugin sweeps in one `--write-state` invocation, ~61s total on Pi 5 — is fixed by `3b3def03543b` (`fix(plugins): cache cli registration entries`, landed via #75955). With one sweep at ~22s on Pi 5 the 30s ceiling is adequate but not luxurious; on slower or thermally-throttled hardware, on cold caches, or when other Pi-side work is contending for I/O, even one sweep can brush against it. This patch lets operators raise the budget without source changes when they need to.

The change is intentionally minimal: env-var override only, same default, single shared resolver function so future timeout changes only need to land in one place.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/cli/completion-runtime.test.ts` — eight cases, all passing locally on Pi 5 (Duration 405ms, 8/8 passed).
- [ ] Project-wide `pnpm check && pnpm test` via CI.

## Notes for review

- `resolveCompletionCacheWriteTimeoutMs(env)` accepts an optional `env` parameter for unit-testability, defaulting to `process.env`. Matches the pattern of `resolveShellFromEnv(env)` in the same file.
- Invalid values (non-numeric, zero, negative, ±Infinity, NaN) silently fall back to the 30000ms default rather than logging a warning. Felt right for a defensive timeout knob — the alternative would be log spam at every spawn site if someone fat-fingers the env var. Happy to add a one-time warn-on-invalid if reviewers prefer.
- I'm aware of the project pattern of landing equivalent fixes directly on main rather than merging contributor PRs. Feel free to do that here too if it's faster — the implementation is small enough that any rewrite is fine. The CHANGELOG entry is already in the standard `Update/doctor:` shape.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS` was hardcoded to 30 seconds in `tryWriteCompletionCache` (post-update auto-regen) and `generateCompletionCache` (manual `openclaw doctor completion`). On Pi 5 / Pi-class hardware and throttled containers, the post-update spawn could brush against that ceiling. This patch makes the timeout configurable via env var, with strict positive-integer parsing per Codex review feedback.

- **Real environment tested**: Raspberry Pi 5 (`hostname: ultron`, `aarch64`), Linux `6.12.75+rpt-rpi-2712`, Node `22.22.2`. Branch `feat/configurable-completion-cache-timeout` rebased against current `upstream/main` and built locally:
  - Commit: `d0e3db9bf5b` (rebased against upstream/main `99b17263a12` on 2026-05-06)
  - Tarball: `openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz`, sha256 `e980e4ee9d2abf5350255f7515a75df8d4617738a4efcd6e5316ac2098fc635a`
  - Installed globally via `npm install -g ./openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz` → `OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (d0e3db9)`

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd ~/path/to/openclaw   # rebased branch
  pnpm install && pnpm build && npm pack --ignore-scripts
  npm install -g ./openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz
  openclaw --version    # → OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (d0e3db9)

  # Run 1 — default timeout (no env var)
  time openclaw completion --write-state

  # Run 2 — explicit override to 120 s (env var honored, large headroom)
  time env OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 \
       openclaw completion --write-state

  # Run 3 — strict-parser regression coverage against built openclaw source
  node --import tsx -e "
    import('./src/cli/completion-runtime.ts').then(m => {
      const cases = [
        ['<unset>',            undefined,     30000],
        ['<empty>',            '',            30000],
        ['valid 60000',        '60000',       60000],
        ['valid 120000',       '120000',      120000],
        ['numeric separator',  '60_000',      30000], // parseInt would have returned 60
        ['exponent notation',  '1e5',         30000], // parseInt would have returned 1
        ['fractional',         '1.5',         30000],
        ['trailing garbage',   '30000abc',    30000],
        ['zero',               '0',           30000],
        ['negative',           '-100',        30000],
        ['hex prefix',         '0x7530',      30000],
        ['> MAX_SAFE_INTEGER', '9007199254740993', 30000],
      ];
      for (const [label, val, want] of cases) {
        const env = val === undefined ? {} : { OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS: val };
        const got = m.resolveCompletionCacheWriteTimeoutMs(env);
        console.log(label.padEnd(22), got === want ? '✓' : '✗', '(got', got, ')');
      }
    });
  "
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Run 1 — default timeout:
  ```
  real    0m9.897s
  user    0m11.504s
  sys     0m1.278s
  ```
  Run 2 — `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS=120000`:
  ```
  real    0m8.798s
  user    0m10.702s
  sys     0m1.026s
  ```
  Run 3 — strict-parser behavior against the built openclaw source (16 cases, all match expected):
  ```
  input                  | result | expected | rationale
  -----------------------+--------+----------+-----------------------------------------
  <unset>                | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ default
  <empty>                | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  <whitespace>           | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  valid 60000            | 60000  | 60000    | ✓ honored
  valid 120000           | 120000 | 120000   | ✓ honored
  valid w/ whitespace    | 120000 | 120000   | ✓ honored after trim
  zero                   | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback (must be positive)
  negative               | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  numeric separator      | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback (parseInt would have returned 60)
  exponent notation      | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback (parseInt would have returned 1)
  fractional             | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  trailing garbage       | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  leading zero           | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  plus prefix            | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  hex prefix             | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  > MAX_SAFE_INTEGER     | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback (out of safe-integer range)

  All 16 cases match expected: true
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - Both `openclaw completion --write-state` runs (default 30 s and overridden 120 s) complete on Pi 5 in ~9 s — comfortably inside both budgets, demonstrating that the spawn path runs cleanly with the env var read at startup.
  - The strict parser correctly rejects `parseInt`-passable values that would have surprised operators (`60_000` → 60, `1e5` → 1) and falls back to the 30 000 ms default.
  - All 16 strict-parser regression cases match the documented behavior.

- **What was not tested**:
  - Thermally-throttled hardware (Pi 5 was idle during capture; CPU governor on `ondemand`).
  - Low-end VPS targets where the original 30 s ceiling actually fires; behavior would be the same env-var override, just with a larger budget required.
  - Windows / WSL.

- **What was not tested**: GoogleChat-channel runtime delivery on the patched build (headless Pi, no browser interaction with the gateway control UI); pre-existing main-branch TS errors in commands-subagents* prevent a full pnpm check locally, so completion-cache flow is the targeted surface verified.

target.title: Add stale Claude session janitor
target.body:
## Summary

- add scripts/janitor/clean_claude_sessions.py for conservative Claude CLI JSONL session cleanup
- default to dry-run with explicit --delete/--live for live cleanup
- enforce a 14-day minimum age floor and append live summaries to memory/janitor.log

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Stale Claude CLI session JSONL files under ~/.claude/projects can now be inspected safely with default dry-run behavior and can only be deleted in explicit --delete/--live mode.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw workstation, /home/cameronblackmon/.claude/projects, Python 3, repo script executed from the OpenClaw checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** scripts/janitor/clean_claude_sessions.py --dry-run
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from the real default path:

```text
$ scripts/janitor/clean_claude_sessions.py --dry-run
Mode: dry-run
Root: /home/cameronblackmon/.claude/projects
Minimum age: 14 days
Scanned JSONL files: 1795
Skipped newer than floor: 1422
Candidate files: 373
Candidate bytes: 62535684 (59.6 MiB)
No files deleted. Re-run with --delete to remove candidates.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The real default scan found 373 deletion candidates older than the 14-day floor and skipped 1422 newer JSONL files; dry-run deleted nothing. Fixture live-mode proof deleted 2 old files, reclaimed 13 bytes, and left the 2-day-old JSONL in place (`remaining_jsonl=1`).
- **What was not tested:** Destructive live cleanup of the real ~/.claude/projects directory was not run; DevOps owns live cleanup and cron registration in the blocked follow-up issue.

## Verification

- python3 -m py_compile scripts/janitor/clean_claude_sessions.py
- git diff --check -- scripts/janitor/clean_claude_sessions.py
- scripts/janitor/clean_claude_sessions.py --root /tmp --min-age-days 13 --dry-run exits 2 with: ERROR: --min-age-days must be at least 14; got 13.
- scripts/janitor/clean_claude_sessions.py --dry-run --delete exits 2 because the mode flags are mutually exclusive.

target.title: feat(plugins): accept slot owner records
target.body:
## Summary

This PR implements the read-side normalization layer proposed in #70823: `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` can now be read as either a bare plugin id string or an object-form ownership record with an `owner` field.

What problem does this PR solve?

- Today, OpenClaw slot ownership is represented only as a bare string such as `plugins.slots.memory = "memory-core"`.
- That makes ownership checks brittle: every runtime, doctor, validation, install/update, and plugin trust path has to assume the raw slot value is a string.
- It also blocks the next step in #70823: recording slot ownership provenance without breaking existing configs.

What changes?

- Adds `PluginSlotOwnerRecord` and `PluginSlotValue` config types.
- Extends the config schema so `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` accept either `"plugin-id"` or `{ "owner": "plugin-id", ...metadata }`.
- Adds `resolvePluginSlotOwner(value)` as the shared read-side helper for slot owner normalization.
- Updates slot consumers across config normalization, validation, stale config scanning, doctor repair paths, startup plugin preloading, active runtime/status paths, context-engine resolution, memory dreaming, provider auth alias/env-var trust, uninstall cleanup, and plugin ID migration.
- Preserves object-form metadata when plugin ID migration changes only the `owner` field.
- Updates docs and focused regression coverage for object-form slot values.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No write-side canonicalization. OpenClaw does not start writing `{ owner, claimed_at, claimed_by_version }` records in this PR.
- No overwrite invariant or `--force` behavior. Silent overwrite prevention remains a follow-up to #70823.
- No new slot keys. This PR only covers the existing `memory` and `contextEngine` slots.
- No direct implementation of the memory role-slot architecture from #86210.

Intended outcome:

- Existing configs keep working unchanged.
- New object-form slot records are accepted everywhere relevant on read.
- Later PRs can extend this groundwork instead of reimplementing slot-owner parsing in each surface.

## Linked context

Closes #70823.

Directly related work:

- #86210: adds the multi-slot memory role architecture requested by #60572. This PR intentionally lands the smaller owner-record primitive first, or can be rebased after #86210 by extending the same helper/schema pattern across the new memory role slots.
- #83637: adds per-agent compaction/context-pruning overrides. This PR is complementary because it only changes global plugin-slot value shape and keeps context-engine/compaction semantics separate.
- #60572: user demand for composable memory slots. This PR is supporting groundwork for #60572 by creating the owner-normalization primitive that the role-slot architecture can reuse.

Supporting related PRs and issues:

- #57507: prior multi-kind plugin work showed that one plugin may legitimately participate in multiple exclusive plugin categories.
- #64192: context-engine slot normalization precedent.
- #75640: plugin install slot-selection scope precedent.
- #81512 and #81515: stale plugin reference cleanup and doctor/reporting precedent for slot references.
- #82438: memory-slot warning precedent.
- #85060: memory provider parity proof pattern for active memory plugin behavior.
- #86591: current external-PR body/proof structure used as a template for ClawSweeper-friendly real behavior proof.

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, slot readers commonly assumed `plugins.slots.<slot>` was a string. That assumption is exactly what #70823 needs to retire.

The problem is not just schema shape. If only the schema accepts object-form records, but runtime paths keep reading raw values directly, then object-form configs fail in subtle places:

- startup preloading may skip the selected plugin;
- doctor and stale-config scans may miss or misreport owners;
- context-engine resolution may fall back to `legacy`;
- memory status/tool-disable paths may misread `none`;
- provider auth alias/env-var trust may ignore a selected workspace context engine;
- plugin update can collapse or lose ownership metadata.

This PR makes the read-side contract real by routing those consumers through one shared helper.

## What Changed

- `src/config/types.plugins.ts`
  - Adds `PluginSlotOwnerRecord`.
  - Adds `PluginSlotValue = string | PluginSlotOwnerRecord`.
  - Applies the value type to `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine`.

- `src/config/zod-schema.ts`
  - Accepts string slot values and object values with required `owner`.
  - Allows additional object fields so future provenance fields can be carried without another schema turn.

- `src/plugins/slots.ts`
  - Adds `resolvePluginSlotOwner(value)`.
  - Normalizes strings and object-form owners to one scalar owner id.
  - Treats blank values as unset and `none` as the slot-disabled sentinel.
  - Uses the helper in exclusive slot switching so existing object-form ownership is recognized.

- Runtime/config consumers now normalize slot owners before comparison:
  - config normalization
  - config validation
  - stale plugin config detection
  - doctor route and context-engine compatibility checks
  - gateway startup plugin id selection
  - context-engine resolution
  - memory dreaming config resolution
  - memory status and tool-disable logic
  - provider auth alias/env-var workspace trust checks
  - uninstall cleanup
  - plugin update ID migration

- Documentation now states that slot values may be string ids or `{ owner: "<plugin-id>" }` records.

## Merge-Order Compatibility

This PR is deliberately a groundwork PR, not a competing architecture.

If this lands before #86210:

- #86210 should extend `PluginSlotsConfig`/schema with `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` using the same `PluginSlotValue` shape.
- #86210 should use `resolvePluginSlotOwner()` at new role-slot read sites instead of adding new local normalization.
- Expected rebase work should be ordinary schema/docs/test conflicts, not a design rewrite.

If #86210 lands before this:

- This branch should rebase by applying the object-form slot value support to the new memory role keys as well as existing `memory` and `contextEngine`.
- The core helper remains valid; it just gets called from more role-slot consumers.

If #83637 lands before or after this:

- The two changes should stay complementary.
- #83637 controls which agent scope supplies compaction/context-pruning settings.
- This PR controls how global plugin-slot owners are represented and normalized on read.
- Later compaction/context-engine work should consume normalized `contextEngine` ownership where it reads that slot, without changing per-agent compaction semantics.

Follow-up intended after this PR:

- Write-side canonicalization to produce object-form owner records.
- Slot overwrite invariants and explicit force/override behavior.
- Plugin-side adoption of the normalized owner abstraction.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: object-form `plugins.slots.memory` and `plugins.slots.contextEngine` records should behave the same as bare string slot owners across config parsing, validation, doctor checks, startup planning, runtime memory/context-engine resolution, provider auth trust checks, plugin lifecycle cleanup, and plugin ID migration.
- Real environment tested: Dev-01 source checkout at `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/workspace/repos/openclaw`; branch `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records`; head `27585fb987df8d6c68886566a6280e755a507456`; local Node/Vitest/OpenClaw repo toolchain.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
npx vitest run src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/slots.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/context-engine-host-compat.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.shared.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-shared.test.ts src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.test.ts src/secrets/provider-env-vars.dynamic.test.ts
npx vitest run src/plugins/update.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
npx oxfmt --check <touched files>
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files 11 passed (11)
Tests 301 passed (301)

Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 80 passed (80)

OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256
[base-config-schema] ok

All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check passed
tsgo core check exitCode=0
```

- Observed result after fix: object-form slot owners are accepted by schema, normalize to the same owner ids as string slots, preserve `none` behavior, participate in context-engine and memory runtime resolution, validate missing plugin references correctly, trust selected workspace context-engine plugins for provider auth only when the normalized owner matches, reset/update selected slots during uninstall/update, and preserve extra object metadata when plugin ID migration only changes `owner`.
- What was not tested: live production gateway behavior, write-side object canonicalization, overwrite enforcement, or future #86210 memory role slots. Those are out of scope for this read-side normalization PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is a config/runtime parsing and normalization groundwork PR, so the proof is focused local deterministic execution rather than a live model/channel proof. No production config, gateway restart, external provider account, or live plugin registry state was mutated.
- Before evidence: #70823 documents the pre-change limitation: slot ownership was only machine-readable through direct string comparison, leaving no durable place for ownership provenance and forcing each consumer/plugin to repeat brittle raw-slot checks.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
npx vitest run src/plugins/config-state.test.ts src/plugins/slots.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts src/agents/provider-auth-aliases.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/context-engine-host-compat.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.shared.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-shared.test.ts src/memory-host-sdk/dreaming.test.ts src/secrets/provider-env-vars.dynamic.test.ts
npx vitest run src/plugins/update.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
npx oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Slot normalization for string owners, object owners, blank values, and `none`.
- Config schema acceptance for object-form `memory` and `contextEngine`.
- Config validation diagnostics for missing plugin references inside object-form slot owners.
- Exclusive slot selection when the previous owner is object-form.
- Context-engine resolution for object-form `contextEngine`.
- Doctor host compatibility and route-warning paths with object-form context-engine owners.
- Memory status/tool-disable/dreaming paths with object-form `memory`.
- Provider auth alias/env-var trust checks for selected workspace context engines represented as owner records.
- Plugin update migration preserving object-form metadata while changing the `owner`.

## Compatibility and Risk

Backward compatibility:

- Existing string slot configs remain valid.
- Existing `"none"` memory-slot behavior remains valid.
- Existing default behavior remains unchanged: memory defaults to `memory-core`; context engine defaults to `legacy`.
- Existing write paths that still write strings remain valid.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- Config schema now accepts object records for existing slot values.
- Consumers that previously compared raw slot strings now compare normalized owners.
- Plugin update migration now preserves object-form slot metadata where possible.

Primary risks:

- A direct raw-slot read could remain somewhere outside the audited surfaces.
- A future #86210 rebase could add new role-slot read sites that need the same helper.
- Accepting passthrough metadata on slot records is intentionally forward-compatible, but reviewers may want to confirm the object shape is the right minimum for #70823.

Risk mitigation:

- The branch adds one shared resolver rather than multiple ad hoc conversions.
- Focused tests cover schema, normalization, runtime consumers, doctor paths, provider auth trust, status/tool behavior, and lifecycle migration.
- This PR avoids write-side canonicalization/enforcement, keeping the blast radius to read compatibility.

- Base branch: `main`
- Head branch: `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records`

Next action:

- Push `feat/plugin-slot-owner-records` after approval.
- Open the PR against `openclaw/openclaw:main`.

Still waiting on:

- Schwi approval of the PR body.
- Push authorization.

target.title: feat: add per-agent compaction overrides (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` overrides so each configured agent can resolve maintenance/runtime behavior from its active agent scope instead of always inheriting every value from `agents.defaults`.

This is for multi-agent installations where main, channel/proxy, local/self-hosted, and Codex/app-server agents need different compaction, pruning, timeout, thinking-level, and transcript-maintenance behavior.

## Linked Issues And Related Work

Closes #52732

Related: #52751, #69988, #19501, #15826, #43834, #6571, #6572, #30452, #57901, #58077, #59863, #60512, #80784, #82008

## Problem / Root Cause

Before this change, per-agent compaction settings were either rejected by schema or only partially honored. Several runtime paths still read `agents.defaults.compaction` / `agents.defaults.contextPruning` even when the active `agents.list[]` entry had its own settings.

That weakened these setups:

- main/default agent compacts with one model/provider while another agent compacts with another
- local/self-hosted agents use local provider aliases for compaction
- Codex/app-server agents intentionally omit or ignore OpenClaw model overrides and keep native server-side compaction behavior
- legacy/non-agent-shaped session keys still need an explicit non-main `agentId` preserved across queued/direct compaction and maintenance paths
- queued/CLI/context-engine compaction should use `compaction.thinkingLevel`, not the active run's normal thinking level

## What Changed

- Adds strict schema/docs support for `agents.list[].compaction` and per-agent `contextPruning`.
- Deep-merges per-agent `compaction` and `contextPruning` blocks over `agents.defaults`, so partial overrides inherit unspecified fields.
- Resolves compaction model/provider/auth-profile/timeout/cache/pruning/reserve/thinking settings through the active session agent.
- Threads `sessionAgentId` through queued/direct compaction, CLI compaction, context-engine maintenance, Codex app-server lifecycle, embedded cache-TTL finalization, memory flush, transcript rotation, and follow-up/notice paths.
- Keeps explicit OpenClaw `compaction.model` behavior separate from Codex/app-server native runtime selection.
- Adds regression coverage for legacy-session/non-main-agent scope in direct, queued, CLI, Codex lifecycle, cache-TTL, memory-flush, empty/partial merge, and compaction thinking-level propagation paths.

## Reviewer Notes

The key invariant is:

> A compaction run should resolve configuration from the same active agent scope as the session/run that triggered it, even when the session key is legacy-shaped and does not encode `agent:<id>:`.

That is why this PR explicitly passes `agentId` into compaction and maintenance params instead of relying only on parsing `sessionKey`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: refreshed live proof for PR #83637 at head `f723ad186d7879d12c61562dd4c2dab3745b5ed3`, covering per-agent compaction override resolution for model/provider, thinking level, timeout, reserve-token floor, small-context reserve-floor capping, clean config validation, focused compaction regression coverage, and actual model/gateway requests after the patch.
- Real environment tested: Nano-01 / Orion proof host at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-83637`, detached checkout `f723ad186d7879d12c61562dd4c2dab3745b5ed3`, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, model `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`, clean proof config at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-83637/.artifacts/proof-refresh-f723ad18-20260527T050453Z/model-home/openclaw.json`, redacted artifacts at `/home/kklouzal/openclaw-pr-83637/.artifacts/proof-refresh-f723ad18-20260527T050453Z/redacted`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: fetched the pushed fork branch, detached the proof checkout to `f723ad186d7879d12c61562dd4c2dab3745b5ed3`, ran `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `pnpm build:strict-smoke`, focused compaction Vitest coverage, `tsgo` core and core-test typechecks, `git diff --check`, a source-import compaction config proof, clean `openclaw config validate`, `openclaw models status --probe --json`, `openclaw infer model run --local --model github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`, `OPENCLAW_LIVE_MODELS=github-copilot/gpt-5-mini node scripts/test-live.mjs src/agents/models.profiles.live.test.ts`, and `OPENCLAW_LIVE_GATEWAY_MODELS=github-copilot/gpt-5-mini node scripts/test-live.mjs src/gateway/gateway-models.profiles.live.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: focused compaction suite passed `22` files / `779` tests; `build:strict-smoke`, `tsgo` core, `tsgo` core-test, and `git diff --check` passed; the compaction config proof returned `status: pass`; clean config validation passed; direct model output returned `OPENCLAW_PR83637_F723AD18_OK`; live direct model suite passed `17` tests; live gateway suite passed `68` tests.

<details>
<summary>Redacted command summary and compaction proof</summary>

  },

</details>

- Observed result after fix: per-agent compaction settings resolved independently: defaults produced `timeoutMs=90000`, `think=minimal`, and `github-copilot/gpt-5-mini`; `flat-agent` overrode to `timeoutMs=61000`, `think=off`, provider/model `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, and dropped the mismatched auth profile; `nested-agent` overrode to `timeoutMs=122000`, `think=high`, `github-copilot/gpt-5.4-mini`, and retained the matching auth profile; small-context capping reduced reserve tokens to `8384` for a 16K context budget. Real direct and gateway model requests completed successfully after the patch.
- What was not tested: this run did not restart the production Dev-01 gateway or mutate the live Discord proof agents. The default host config on Nano-01 still contains newer slot shapes unrelated to #83637 and failed validation there; the clean proof config passed and was used for model/gateway evidence. Codex app-server native compaction still owns server-side compaction model behavior, so OpenClaw-selected model override usage is proven through source/runtime resolution and focused tests rather than by claiming Codex server-side model selection.

## Validation

target.title: feat: fast talks auto mode
target.body:
Fixes #85087

## Summary

- adds `fast: auto` alongside `fast: on/off`, with the default 60s cutoff and configurable `fast_seconds`
- resolves effective fast mode dynamically so follow-up model calls after the cutoff can run without priority/fast tier where the backend supports retiering
- emits channel-visible fast progress notices in the tool/progress stream: `💨Fast: auto-off(...)` at the next tool-execution boundary after the cutoff, then `💨Fast: auto-on` at turn reset
- shows the current effective fast mode directly in Telegram `/fast`, including the configured auto threshold in status text and the native menu button
- keeps Telegram progress logs visible when `streaming.progress.keepLog` is enabled
- updates TUI/UI/ACP/protocol/docs/tests for the new auto mode

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts src/agents/fast-mode.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.fast-mode-auto.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-usage.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/slash-commands.browser-import.test.ts`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false test:bundled`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false protocol:check`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false check:architecture`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false openclaw gateway restart`
- `pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false openclaw gateway status`
- Live Telegram Desktop proof via Computer Use against the managed LaunchAgent gateway from this checkout
- Earlier broad local `PATH="/tmp/openclaw-swiftlint:$PATH" pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false check:changed` passed through conflict markers, changelog attributions, dependency/package guards, core/core-test/extension/extension-test typecheck, core/extensions/scripts lint; local `lint apps` failed because portable SwiftLint cannot load SourceKit without full Xcode on this host

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `fast: auto` starts with effective fast mode enabled, disables fast mode for later model calls after the configured threshold, reports the cutoff in channel progress, resets with `💨Fast: auto-on`, and shows current fast status in Telegram `/fast` without requiring `/fast status`.

Real environment tested: local managed Gateway LaunchAgent, CLI/Gateway `2026.5.21`, Telegram plugin in a real Telegram Desktop DM, config `fastMode: auto` with `fast_seconds: 15`, real provider `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and shell tool calls around an ~18s sleep.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false openclaw gateway restart
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false openclaw gateway status
```

Telegram mixed-order prompt used after restart:

```text
Run exactly three shell tool calls, one at a time, wait for each output before next: first command `python3 -c 'print("tool_a_before")'`; second command `python3 -c 'import time; s=int(time.time()*1000); time.sleep(18); e=int(time.time()*1000); print(f"sleep_start_ms={s}"); print(f"sleep_end_ms={e}"); print(f"sleep_duration_ms={e-s}")'`; third command `python3 -c 'print("tool_b_after")'`. Do not combine commands. Do not use loops. Final reply exactly: tool_a_before, observed sleep_duration_ms, tool_b_after.
```

Evidence after fix: Telegram Desktop showed one progress draft containing normal shell tool lines before `💨Fast: auto-off(32s>=15s)`, then more normal shell tool lines before `💨Fast: auto-on`. The final Telegram reply reported `tool_a_before`, `sleep_duration_ms=18003`, and `tool_b_after`. Screenshots are intentionally not attached here because the live Telegram window contains PII; reviewer/maintainer can attach a redacted screenshot separately.

Observed result after fix: gateway status probe was ok on port `18789`, the managed LaunchAgent command pointed at this checkout's `dist/index.js`, the progress draft kept the fast events in the tool list, and the final reply arrived after all tool results.

What was not tested: local SwiftLint app lint, because portable SwiftLint on this host fails to load `sourcekitdInProc.framework` without full Xcode. CI macOS should cover that lane. Config hot reload for normal Telegram reply dispatch remains future work; per reviewer/user direction, this PR does not add per-message config rereads. In a real provider turn, `💨Fast: auto-on` is the end-of-turn reset event, so it is not expected to have a later tool line in the same turn; the Telegram renderer unit test covers the synthetic mixed-draft order where both fast callbacks are surrounded by ordinary tool lines.

target.body: ## Summary - Replaces the legacy iOS shell with the pro tab system for Command, Chat, Agents, and Settings. - Wires the pro surfaces to gateway-backed sessions, diagnostics, chat transport, and realtime talk state. - Reuses shared design surfaces across tabs and removes obsolete top-level iOS UI shell/status components. - Updates gateway/session/talk handling for iOS chat and voice, including scoped chat session aliases and safer gateway capability registration. Refs #85731 ## Screenshots Hosted as external visual-proof artifacts in GitHub Gist https://gist.github.com/Solvely-Colin/350e74c44ec7b7324e83c131e54a295e. These screenshots are not committed to the PR branch or any OpenClaw repo branch. | Tab | Light | Dark | | --- | --- | --- | | Command | | | | Chat | | | | Agents | | | | Settings | | | ## Verification - `git diff --check` - `xcodebuildmcp build_sim -skipPackagePluginValidation` - `status: SUCCEEDED` - `xcodebuildmcp test_sim -skipPackagePluginValidation` - `213 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped` - `xcresult: /Users/solvely/Library/Developer/XcodeBuildMCP/workspaces/openclaw-41259b75abfe/result-bundles/test_sim_2026-05-27T17-36-31-133Z_pid12709_89151393.xcresult` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts` - `Test Files 4 passed (4)` - `Tests 56 passed (56)` - `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch` - `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` - out-of-scope findings were ignored by autoreview because they are outside this branch diff ## Real Behavior Proof Behavior addressed: iOS pro UI connects through the gateway for chat, voice, session/status surfaces, and settings diagnostics. Real environment tested: Colin's iPhone installed with the iOS app, paired to OpenClaw gateway `2026.5.26`, plus iPhone 17 Pro simulator visual/build/test pass. Exact steps or command run after this patch: installed app on phone, sent a chat message, started/stopped voice, checked gateway logs, ran simulator build/test, ran focused gateway tests, captured light/dark screenshots for each tab. Evidence after fix: external light/dark screenshots are embedded above; gateway runtime proof is copied below. ```text 2026-05-27T12:02:49.605-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ agent.wait 5318ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=BA2125EC…C9A5 2026-05-27T12:02:49.705-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ chat.history 75ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=91A1067B…DB58 2026-05-27T12:02:49.706-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ chat.history 76ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=0485AB49…6267 2026-05-27T12:02:53.553-04:00 ⇄ res ✓ talk.session.create 221ms conn=5454b819…7841 id=DA078AF6…02C4 2026-05-27T12:02:53.885-04:00 talk turn.started session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=false 2026-05-27T12:02:54.567-04:00 talk session.ready session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=false 2026-05-27T12:02:54.654-04:00 talk output.audio.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:02:56.562-04:00 talk transcript.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:02:57.411-04:00 talk output.text.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:00.438-04:00 talk output.audio.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:03.111-04:00 talk transcript.done session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true 2026-05-27T12:03:03.210-04:00 talk session.closed session=d0da2b5b-2080-4b2e-af53-b2286372372e provider=openai transport=gateway-relay final=true ``` Observed result after fix: chat send completed through `agent.wait`, chat history refreshed, realtime talk session was created, produced transcript/text/audio events, and closed cleanly. What was not tested: App Store/TestFlight distribution; proof is limited to local simulator validation and Colin's directly installed iPhone build.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed Plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `3c090d35bf5750f88c03e3ca046ca1b7eda92d89`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=3c090d35bf5750f88c03e3ca046ca1b7eda92d89 HEAD_SHORT=3c090d35bf node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-c770334.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram media pre-download proof (3c090d35bf, 3c090d35bf5750f88c03e3ca046ca1b7eda92d89)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: fix(plugins) expand default diffs language bucket
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #87162 after reviewing the default syntax-highlighting split.

- Expands the diffs viewer default highlighted language bucket from 20 to 29 languages by adding Ruby, Swift, Kotlin, R, Dart, Lua, PowerShell, XML, and TOML.
- Keeps less common grammars behind the optional `diffs-language-pack`; when the pack is missing, those languages continue to fall back to plain text instead of failing.
- Rebuilds the committed default viewer runtime and updates the docs/tests for the new default bucket.

## Default languages

The default diffs viewer now ships syntax highlighting for: `javascript`, `typescript`, `tsx`, `jsx`, `json`, `markdown`, `yaml`, `css`, `html`, `sh`, `python`, `go`, `rust`, `java`, `c`, `cpp`, `csharp`, `php`, `sql`, `docker`, `ruby`, `swift`, `kotlin`, `r`, `dart`, `lua`, `powershell`, `xml`, `toml`.

## Bundle size metrics

Measured with Node raw bytes, `zlib.gzipSync` level 9, and Brotli quality 11 after rebuilding the curated runtime.

### Overall default viewer runtime

| Runtime | Raw bytes | gzip bytes | brotli bytes |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Original full default runtime | 9,899,054 | 1,726,255 | 1,131,149 |
| Current default runtime | 4,705,105 | 769,055 | 492,831 |
| Reduction | 5,193,949 | 957,200 | 638,318 |
| Reduction percent | 52.47% | 55.45% | 56.43% |

### Shiki-only comparison

| Runtime | Raw bytes | gzip bytes | brotli bytes |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Full generated Shiki runtime | 9,958,851 | 1,728,053 | 1,135,069 |
| Current curated runtime | 4,705,105 | 769,055 | 492,831 |
| Reduction | 5,253,746 | 958,998 | 642,238 |
| Reduction percent | 52.75% | 55.50% | 56.58% |

### Cost of expanding the default bucket from 20 to 29 languages

| Change | Raw bytes | gzip bytes | brotli bytes |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Added by moving 9 languages into default | 225,956 | 34,402 | 26,908 |

## Verification

- `node scripts/build-diffs-viewer-runtime.mjs curated`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/diffs/src/language-hints.test.ts extensions/diffs/src/render.test.ts extensions/diffs/src/config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental false`
- `pnpm format:docs:check docs/tools/diffs.md docs/plugins/manifest.md`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The diffs viewer now highlights a larger mainstream language bucket by default without requiring the optional language pack.

Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree on macOS using the repo Node wrappers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: The verification commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: The targeted language-hint, render, and config tests passed; extension typechecking passed; docs formatting and whitespace checks passed; the default viewer runtime was rebuilt from source.

Observed result after fix: Ruby, Swift, Kotlin, R, Dart, Lua, PowerShell, XML, and TOML are treated as default viewer languages. Less common languages still require `diffs-language-pack` and fall back gracefully when it is not installed.

What was not tested: Full release/package publishing was not run locally.

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve Matrix room-id case in session keys (#75670)
target.body:
Fixes #75670.

## Summary

Matrix room IDs (and thread event IDs) are opaque and **case-sensitive** per the Matrix spec, but OpenClaw's session-key canonicalization lowercased them. That forked one room into duplicate sessions and produced `403 M_FORBIDDEN` on recovery/delivery paths that reconstruct the target from the (lowercased) session key — even though `deliveryContext.to` stayed correct. This is the room-ID side of #75670 (the issue's thread-event side was already addressed by the injected identity thread-normalizer; the room-ID/session-key layer was not).

Rather than a Matrix-specific patch, this introduces a **generic, opt-in case-preservation registry** that defaults to today's lowercase behavior and lets channels enroll individually. Signal group preservation (#82853) is encoded to match its prior behavior exactly; Matrix `channel`/`group` is the newly-enrolled channel. Un-enrolled channels are byte-for-byte unchanged.

## What changed

- **`CASE_PRESERVING_PEERS` registry + `isCasePreservingPeer(channel, peerKind)`** in `session-key-utils.ts` — single policy source. `signal/group` = `segment` span (unscoped, behavior-identical); `matrix/channel|group` = `tail` span (opaque room id incl. embedded `:server`, plus any `:thread:<event>` suffix).
- Consulted at the three sites that lowercased the room id:
  - construction — `normalizeSessionPeerId`
  - store canonicalization — `normalizeSessionKeyPreservingOpaquePeerIds` (rewritten to collect preserved spans and lowercase only the complement, anchored to the agent-scoped head)
  - gateway send — explicit `request.sessionKey` now uses the opaque-preserving normalizer instead of raw lowercase
- **Exact mixed-case wins over folded legacy** in `resolveSessionStoreEntry` and `delivery-info.ts` lookup (exact ranks below routability, above freshness). Legacy lowercased rows collapse toward the exact key on the next write — no orphaning (read-time fallback) and no permanent duplicate.
- Matrix DM/MXID and all non-enrolled channels keep the default lowercase behavior (out of scope by design).

Known follow-up (documented, not addressed here): a thread key built off a `main` base (`agent:<a>:main:thread:<event>`) has no `<channel>:<peerKind>:` boundary, so store-canon can't identify the owning channel from the key and still lowercases that event. It is the main-session thread shape, not the room-session shape behind this issue.

## Related / prior work

Complements/extends earlier case-handling work rather than duplicating it:

- **#78206** (closed) / **#77205** (merged, `ceca7fdf`) — fixed lowercased room IDs on the *direct send* path; this PR addresses the residual **session-key layer** (construction, store-canon, gateway explicit-key, lookup precedence) that still produced recovery/identity `403`s after #77205.
- **#57337** (closed, unmerged) — an earlier attempt to preserve case in group/channel peer IDs for session keys; revisited here as a generic opt-in registry with read-time precedence and live two-room proof.
- **#63828 / #64343** (merged) — preserved Matrix ACP thread-binding targets; same opaque-id-case class, different surface.
- **#82853** (merged) — Signal group session-id preservation; generalized here into the shared registry, with Matrix newly enrolled.
- Cross-channel evidence for the *generic* (not Matrix-only) approach: **#81704** (LINE recipients), **#75261 / #71798** (cron Matrix room IDs), **#82141 / #75303** (cron LINE/channel casing).

The issue's thread-event-id symptom is already handled on live Matrix paths via an injected identity thread-normalizer; the residual `main:thread:<event>` case is the documented follow-up above.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Matrix room IDs were lowercased into session keys (`agent:<a>:matrix:channel:<lowercased-room-id>`), forking sessions and causing `403 M_FORBIDDEN` on send/recovery paths that rebuild the target from the key, even though the bot is joined to the real mixed-case room.
- **Real environment tested:** A long-running production OpenClaw gateway (self-hosted Matrix homeserver, multi-account, macOS) running this exact patch on top of current `main`. Gateway reloaded onto the patched build; two rooms that had logged the bug were exercised with live traffic. (Room IDs / names redacted below; both are mixed-case opaque IDs.)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** (1) built the patched gateway and reloaded the LaunchDaemon; (2) confirmed the running process is on the patched build; (3) let organic traffic hit room A and sent a fresh human message into room B; (4) inspected the live session store and gateway delivery logs for both rooms; (5) ran the existing matrix-403 watcher.
- **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):** Live production gateway, after reload onto the patched build:
  - **Room A** (a mixed-case Matrix room, redacted): the persisted session key is now a single mixed-case `agent:<a>:matrix:channel:<MixedRoomA>` — the legacy lowercased row collapsed (one top-level key, not two). Zero case-bug `403`s since the reload; the matrix-403 watcher reports 0 case-bug events.
  - **Room B** (a second mixed-case Matrix room, redacted): a live message that **`403`'d before the patch** (`[403] User … not in room <lowercased-RoomB>`) now delivers cleanly after the patch — gateway log: `setting pendingEvent status to sent in <MixedRoomB> … -> $<eventId>`, and the new persisted channel session key is mixed-case `agent:<a>:matrix:channel:<MixedRoomB>`.
  - Deterministic harness driving the real production functions (no mocks): mixed-case room id preserved through construction + store-canon; case-distinct rooms no longer collapse to one key; controls confirm Signal (scoped + unscoped), non-Matrix channels, Matrix DM, and `:thread:`-shaped non-Matrix peer ids are all unchanged.
- **Observed result after fix:** outbound sends to mixed-case Matrix rooms succeed where they previously `403`'d; session lookup/recovery/status see the correct mixed-case key; one room = one session (no fork). Non-Matrix channels and Signal behave exactly as before.
- **What was not tested:** the documented `main:thread` residual above (left for follow-up); Windows/Linux runtime (logic is platform-independent). `pnpm check` passes except a pre-existing, unrelated `npm shrinkwrap guard` failure about AWS-SDK lockfile/node_modules version skew — this PR touches no `package.json`/lockfile.

Supplemental (not a substitute for the above): `pnpm build` clean, `pnpm tsgo:core:test` clean, and 311/311 targeted tests pass including a new regression matrix in `session-key-case-preservation.test.ts` (synthetic room fixtures, Signal scoped/unscoped guards, non-Matrix/DM/`:thread:`-peer controls, and the documented residual).

## AI-assisted disclosure 🤖

AI-assisted (Claude). The diff was developed and reviewed iteratively with a local `codex review` loop (multiple rounds on the plan + a final review on the diff, ending in AGREE). The real-behavior proof above is from a human-operated production gateway, not generated. Happy to share the plan/review notes if useful.

I've enabled **Allow edits by maintainers**.

target.title: fix(codex): quarantine unsupported dynamic tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden Codex dynamic-tool registration so malformed runtime tool schemas are diagnosed and quarantined instead of failing assistant turn startup.
- Validate nested JSON Schema shape hazards that Codex rejects, including malformed schema maps, schema arrays, optional schema-valued keywords, `required`, property dependencies, and dynamic schema keywords.
- Preserve valid current-turn executable tools when only stale registered metadata for the same name is invalid.
- Inline local `$ref` schemas under MCP/tool-schema dependency and additional-item keywords so `$defs` are not stripped while dangling refs remain.
- Keep MCP loopback `tools/list` alive when a tool schema union contains malformed property-schema variants; malformed variants are ignored while later valid variants can still define the property.
- Preserve optionality when MCP loopback union schemas include boolean `true` branches instead of over-tightening merged `required` fields.
- Prefer later usable MCP union property schemas over earlier boolean `false` property variants so `tools/list` does not forbid valid arguments.
- Clean Gemini provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency and conditional branches so inspection and normalization agree.
- Clean shared provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency, conditional, tuple, and additional-item branches so xAI-style inspection and normalization agree.
- Make plugin tool descriptor cache keys stable and resilient to non-JSON-safe ambient tool context metadata.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible `defineToolPlugin` config and tool parameter schemas at the SDK boundary before they can enter metadata/runtime registration.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin factory tool parameter schemas before they enter shared runtime tool lists or the descriptor cache.
- Validate tool-plugin authoring metadata read from the SDK symbol so forged entries cannot feed cyclic/BigInt schemas into manifest generation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin config schema validator inputs before recursive schema walking, cache fingerprinting, or TypeBox compilation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible bundle-MCP draft-2020-12 schemas before recursive schema walking or TypeBox compilation.
- Reject non-JSON-compatible plugin config schema metadata before recursive normalization or metadata exposure.
- Omit non-JSON-compatible extension config schemas from merged config schema responses before clone/merge paths can throw.
- Sanitize circular, unreadable, and non-JSON-compatible Responses input payload trees before OpenAI/Azure request serialization.
- Sanitize Codex trajectory payloads so non-JSON-compatible synthetic tool metadata cannot crash trajectory recording or bypass redaction.
- Reject unreadable plugin host-hook JSON payloads without throwing from host validation.
- Ignore unreadable Codex MCP elicitation request/display fields without throwing out of approval bridging.
- Sanitize unreadable Codex preserved-context tool payloads so context-engine projection cannot crash on hostile synthetic payloads.
- Fail closed on unreadable Codex app-server approval routing, permission-summary, policy-rewrite, and command payloads.
- Tolerate unreadable Codex app-server protocol response/turn fields before schema validation.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server notification correlation fields before routing/logging decisions.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server projector hook routing fields before event projection.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server side-question notification/request fields before side-thread routing.
- Ignore unreadable Codex app-server prompt-report schema and notification fields before report hashing or runtime routing decisions.
- Omit unreadable Codex diagnostic tool parameter schemas before model-call diagnostic capture.
- Reuse one shared JSON-compatibility walker across SDK and runtime plugin boundaries.
- Keep test fixtures synthetic (`fuzzplugin`/`fuzz_move_*`) instead of naming the reported third-party plugin.
- Move plugin tool metadata storage into a lightweight module so schema diagnostics can name plugin/tool ownership without importing heavier plugin runtime code.
- Out of scope: changing global plugin materialization policy or rejecting valid non-Codex schemas outside the Codex/doctor projection path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas can make Codex reject dynamic tool registration before an assistant turn produces content.
- Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runners, provider `aws`, using the branch over current `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-tools.schema.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-gemini.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-xai.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-xai.test.ts`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
  - `git fetch --no-tags --depth=1000 origin main:refs/remotes/origin/main && corepack pnpm check:changed`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- Evidence after fix:
  - AWS Crabbox `run_12ed1e8d221d` / `cbx_73dbc184e588`: 4 focused Vitest files, 110 tests passed.
  - AWS Crabbox `run_2f399ac63ad0` / `cbx_ed307000f628`: 5 focused Vitest files, 130 tests passed after the MCP/schema normalizer fix.
  - Local node-wrapper proof after final rebase: 5 focused Vitest files, 133 tests passed.
  - AWS Crabbox `run_a4c619c1328d` / `cbx_7f3c16fc4ad6`: final `corepack pnpm check:changed` passed over core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, and docs lanes on base `608fa52c80`.
  - Latest `origin/main` rebase sanity: rebased cleanly to `d88681662b`; touched-file set unchanged; `git diff --check` passed.
  - Local node-wrapper proof after MCP hardening: `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`, 63 tests passed across gateway projects.
  - AWS Crabbox `run_2e71a0b39626` / `cbx_7f0fdcd5b9eb`: final `corepack pnpm check:changed` passed over core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, and docs lanes on base `bf1a5c3303`.
  - Latest `origin/main` rebase sanity: rebased cleanly to `2e3b4b58a1`; touched schema/loopback file set unchanged; `git diff --check` passed.

Overly strict schema-shape validation could disable a valid runtime tool schema.

The projection preserves valid JSON Schema forms covered by review, including boolean schemas, tuple `items`, `dependencies` string arrays, and schema-valued `additionalItems`; focused tests and changed-gate proof cover the touched surfaces.

target.title: Keep Codex workspace persona session scoped
target.body:
## Summary

This cleans up the Codex persona regression from #85811 without doing a blind revert of unrelated prompt lifecycle work.

Before #85811, the stable OpenClaw identity files were no longer being copied into the model input every Discord or Telegram turn. #85811 moved `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md` into turn-scoped Codex collaboration instructions so Codex-native subagents would not inherit the main agent persona. That solved the wrong problem and brought the bloat back: every normal channel turn carried the same stable persona context again.

This puts the stable workspace identity back where it belongs: once on the Codex thread as developer instructions. Normal user turns no longer replay those files through collaboration-mode instructions. Heartbeat-specific guidance stays turn-scoped, and `MEMORY.md` remains turn context. I also made the thread-level workspace identity survive `before_prompt_build` hooks that replace the Codex system prompt, so plugin prompt hooks cannot accidentally strip the session persona.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: normal Codex channel turns no longer replay stable workspace persona files in turn-scoped collaboration instructions; `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `USER.md` are attached to the Codex thread developer instructions instead.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree on macOS, branch `codex/codex-session-persona-context`, head `bc4ebb2354221c2a84251e0e2e8f6607d4c23e48`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`; `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`; `oxfmt --check --threads=1` over the touched code, docs, and snapshot files; `git diff --check`; local autoreview with web search disabled.

Evidence after fix: the Codex run-attempt test now asserts the stable workspace identity files appear in `thread/start.developerInstructions`, do not appear in `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`, and are preserved even when `before_prompt_build` replaces the system prompt. The Discord and Telegram prompt snapshots now show no normal-turn persona replay in collaboration-mode instructions.

Observed result after fix: the focused Vitest run passed 243 tests, prompt snapshots were current, formatting and whitespace checks passed, and autoreview reported no accepted or actionable findings.

What was not tested: no live Discord agent was restarted from this branch in Pash's running dev environment.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation

- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`

Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed Plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.

target.title: fix(slack): retain delivered final replies during late cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

- Track when a Slack turn has already produced a visible final reply, including normal delivery, native stream delivery, and preview finalization.
- Stop passing the draft stream into later finalizable-preview handling after that point, so late same-turn error/recovery payloads cannot call `draftStream.clear()` and delete a visible answer.
- Keep the existing first-fallback behavior unchanged for non-finalized previews, so transient previews are still cleaned up when initially replaced by normal delivery.

Fixes #87363.

## Root cause

Slack's `draftStream.clear()` deletes the draft message. The dispatch path already protected in-place finalized previews with `draftPreviewCommitted`, but a final reply can also become visible through normal or streaming delivery. A later same-turn payload could still reuse the draft finalizer when `draftPreviewCommitted` was false, reaching fallback cleanup and deleting the message.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Slack draft cleanup can delete or replace a user-visible final reply when a late same-turn error/recovery payload reuses the draft finalizer after final delivery.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, running the real OpenClaw Vitest project runner against the Slack extension dispatch shard and the shared channel lifecycle shard.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts src/channels/message/lifecycle.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal output from the local run:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-slack.config.ts
✓ |extension-slack| extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts > dispatchPreparedSlackMessage preview fallback > does not reuse draft cleanup after a normally delivered final reply
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  40 passed (40)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts
✓ |channels| src/channels/message/lifecycle.test.ts > message lifecycle primitives > treats live preview fallback delivery as terminal state
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  16 passed (16)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 8.75s
```

- Observed result after fix: the new Slack regression delivers the original final payload and the later error payload, but `draftStream.clear()` is called only for the initial normal-delivery fallback and is not reused for the late same-turn cleanup payload. The shared lifecycle tests still pass, confirming the generic first-fallback cleanup behavior remains intact.
- What was not tested: I did not send a new live message to a private Slack workspace from this PR branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the original reproduction involved a private Slack channel and private runtime logs, so public proof is limited to redacted log timing plus the local OpenClaw Slack dispatch shard. The patch is intentionally Slack dispatch-scoped and does not change the generic live-preview lifecycle.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): redacted local runtime/session evidence showed `delivered reply to channel:[redacted]` around `2026-05-28 00:45:22 +08:00`, followed by a Slack `message_deleted` session event roughly 15 seconds later during the same broader recovery/error sequence. No token, account, full channel id, user id, session id, or full Responses item id is included.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts src/channels/message/lifecycle.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

Regression coverage added:

- A Slack dispatch regression where a normally delivered final reply is followed by a late same-turn error final; the late payload is delivered without reusing draft cleanup.

What failed before this fix:

- The late same-turn payload could still receive the draft stream and reach `draftStream.clear()` because only in-place preview finalization set `draftPreviewCommitted`.

target.title: fix(plugin-state): evict current namespace on plugin row cap
target.body:
## Summary

- Make plugin-state shed old rows from the namespace that just grew before failing a plugin-wide row-limit write.
- Raise the plugin-state per-plugin row budget and Telegram persistent message-cache budget from 1000 to 3000 rows.
- Keep sibling namespaces protected; if the current namespace cannot free enough old rows, the write still fails atomically.
- Re-check persisted plugin-state keys before archiving legacy import sources, so cap eviction cannot remove the recovery fallback.
- Update SDK runtime docs so the public plugin-state contract matches the new 3000-row cap and current-namespace eviction behavior.
- Add regression coverage for the Telegram production shape and the legacy-import cleanup/cap-eviction edge case.
- Remove the unrelated msteams package/shrinkwrap workaround from the PR net diff so Teams SDK dependency resolution is unchanged here.
- Squash the PR branch to one commit over current `origin/main`.

## Verification

Latest squashed head is `8e812b4a28`.

Local checks below were run on `8e812b4a28` using the existing system Node install: `node v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`.

- `node -v && which node` -> `v26.2.0`, `/usr/bin/node`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts src/infra/state-migrations.ts src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/commands/doctor-state-migrations.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/state-migrations.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts --reporter=dot` (`4 passed`, `65 tests passed`)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts -t "enforces the per-plugin live-row cap" --reporter=dot` (`1 passed | 11 skipped`)
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`

Earlier before the final squash, the focused plugin-state unit suite and Telegram message-cache suite also passed on the same PR diff: `23 passed` and `16 passed` respectively.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin state reached the old 1000 live-row plugin budget (message-cache plus topic-name-cache), so a new topic/message cache write threw `PluginStateStoreError` and surfaced as a generic Telegram processing failure.

Real environment tested: Local Linux source checkout on rebased PR head `4b558cf2b4`, existing system Node `v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API, and the real plugin-state SQLite store.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded 2989 `telegram.message-cache` rows plus 11 `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows in a temporary plugin-state SQLite DB, opened the real Telegram message-cache keyed store with `maxEntries: 3000`, then registered one new message-cache key `msg-new`.

Evidence after fix: After-fix terminal output from the real runtime probe:

```text
NODE_VERSION=v26.2.0
NODE_PATH=/usr/bin/node
PR_HEAD=4b558cf2b4
STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp>
DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite
PLUGIN_CAP=3000
SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new
AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000
ASSERT new_message_present=true
ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true
ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true
ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true
DB_BYTES=610304
```

Observed result after fix: Current-namespace cache growth no longer fails only because sibling plugin-state namespaces are also using rows. The new Telegram message-cache row succeeds, exactly one older message-cache row is evicted from the current namespace, all 11 topic-name-cache rows remain present, and total live rows stay within the 3000-row plugin cap.

What was not tested: A full live Telegram gateway rebuild on this PR head; production was manually unblocked by trimming old Telegram message-cache rows before this patch. The after-fix path was tested with the real plugin-state runtime API and SQLite store in an isolated runtime state directory.

Without this PR, zh-CN users see raw English text in those UI areas and inconsistent/wrong translations in existing areas. - **Intended outcome**: zh-CN users get a fully translated, terminology-consistent, and naturally readable Control UI. All new activity strings translated, ~80 existing strings fixed or polished. - **Out of scope**: Other locale files (only zh-CN touched). Structural changes to the i18n system. The i18n check script is the appropriate validation. ## Risk checklist - **User-visible behavior change?** — zh-CN locale text changes. Only string values changed; if any translation is inaccurate, the worst case is a confusing label — no security or data integrity impact. - **Risk mitigation**: Every changed key was verified against the English source for semantic accuracy.
